page: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Daldsv7 family. It is powered by dedicated AMD processors managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 128 dedicated vCPUs paired with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration includes 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options. The hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff, offering a high compute core count alongside substantial local storage capacity, but with a low memory-to-core ratio. There are no integrated graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server is structured for workloads requiring high parallel processing capabilities and large local storage volumes without the overhead of high memory allocations. This makes the instance suitable for compute-heavy tasks, large-scale local data processing, and distributed database nodes where memory requirements do not exceed the 2.0 GB per core threshold.
description: Microsoft Azure D128alds_v7 is an x86_64 server instance within the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD processors, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile represents a tradeoff optimized for high-density compute and large local storage capacity rather than memory-intensive operations. Qualitatively, it offers resource density suited for parallel processing tasks that do not demand extensive RAM. It is designed for workloads such as compute-heavy applications, large-scale local data processing, and database nodes.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
